reasoning: The formalization faithfully captures the informal statement. MvPolynomial N k is isomorphic to k[X_1, X_2, ...] under re-indexing. The sequence m : N -> N with StrictMono m captures 'm_1 < m_2 < ...' under standard index shift. The ideals ideal_x k (Set.Ioc (m n) (m (n+1))) correctly generate the primes from the informal. StrictMono on consecutive differences is equivalent to the informal's growth condition. The compl_all definition uses Submonoid.closure on the complement of the union of primes; since the ideals are prime (generated by subsets of variables), the complement is already multiplicatively closed, so closure equals the complement. ringKrullDim = top correctly expresses infinite Krull dimension.
